words.hint: Markup language used to create web pages
words.educational: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard language for creating web pages. It defines the structure and content of websites.

words.hint: Repeats a block of code multiple times
words.educational: Loops are fundamental programming constructs that allow you to execute code repeatedly. Common types include for, while, and do-while loops.

words.hint: Data structure that stores multiple values in order
words.educational: Arrays are ordered collections of elements. They allow you to store and access multiple values using numerical indices.

words.hint: Blueprint for creating objects in OOP
words.educational: In Object-Oriented Programming, a class is a template that defines the properties and methods that objects will have.

words.hint: Data type for text and characters
words.educational: Strings are sequences of characters used to represent text in programming. They're one of the most commonly used data types.

words.hint: Finding and fixing errors in code
words.educational: Debugging is the process of identifying, analyzing, and removing bugs or errors from computer programs.

words.hint: Reusable block of code that performs a task
words.educational: Functions are self-contained blocks of code that perform specific tasks. They help organize code and promote reusability.
Variables store data values that can be used and modified throughout a program. They're essential for dynamic programming.
Syntax refers to the set of rules that define how programs are written in a programming language. Correct syntax is crucial for code to run.

words.hint: Data type with only true or false values
words.educational: Booleans are logical data types that can only be true or false. They're essential for conditional statements and logical operations.
